sections.heading: What We Make
paragraphs: The Rainbow Hub is a lightweight quiz website built around short, focused challenges. The goal is simple: give visitors something quick, polished, and mentally engaging that works well on mobile and desktop.

Our quiz formats include IQ-style pattern recognition, verbal reasoning, number puzzles, academic-style logic, attention checks, and general knowledge prompts. The experience is intentionally fast: clear cards, direct questions, quick feedback, and results that are easy to understand.

sections.heading: Our Quiz Philosophy
list: Questions should be readable on a phone without pinching or zooming.
Answer choices should be clear and avoid unnecessary trick wording.
Results should feel rewarding without pretending to be official credentials.
Academic-style quizzes should be challenging but transparent about their limitations.
Pages should load quickly and keep the main experience usable before anything decorative.

sections.heading: What Our Scores Mean
paragraphs: Scores are based only on the questions in the quiz you take. A high score can mean you did well on that challenge. It does not prove an official IQ, academic ability, admissions readiness, or professional qualification.

Pass rates and result labels are editorial benchmarks designed to make the quiz more fun. They should not be read as scientific population statistics unless a quiz clearly says it uses validated data.

sections.heading: Advertising and Free Access
paragraphs: The site may use advertising and measurement scripts to keep quizzes free to access. We aim to balance monetization with speed, readability, and a clean user experience. Rewarded ads may appear around optional result or review moments where available.

sections.heading: Editorial Standards
paragraphs: We aim to avoid harmful claims, misleading score labels, or language that makes a playful quiz look like a formal diagnosis or official examination. If you spot a question that is unclear, incorrect, or unfairly worded, please contact us.

contact.title: Contact The Rainbow Hub
contact.updated: Use this page for editorial, privacy, accessibility, or support questions.

sections.heading: General Contact
paragraphs: For general support, editorial questions, accessibility issues, privacy requests, quiz corrections, advertising questions, or technical problems, use the email address shown below. Please include the page URL and enough detail for us to understand the issue.

sections.heading: Privacy Requests
paragraphs: If you are contacting us about privacy rights, include the country or state you are writing from, the browser/device used, and the request you want us to review. Do not send sensitive documents unless we specifically ask for them.

sections.heading: Quiz Corrections
paragraphs: To report a question issue, include the quiz name, question number, the answer you selected, and why you believe the prompt or explanation should be reviewed.

sections.heading: Advertising or Technical Issues
paragraphs: For ad, loading, or display problems, include the page URL, approximate time, device, browser, and screenshots if available.

sections.heading: Our Commitment
paragraphs: The Rainbow Hub aims to provide quiz pages that are usable across common devices, screen sizes, browsers, and input methods. We work toward clear contrast, keyboard-friendly controls, readable text, and semantic page structure.

sections.heading: Current Features
list: Responsive layouts for mobile and desktop screens.
Semantic links, headings, buttons, and navigation landmarks.
High-contrast answer states for correct and incorrect responses.
Text-based quiz content wherever possible, with visual prompts kept simple.
Keyboard-operable buttons and links for core quiz flows.

sections.heading: Known Limitations
paragraphs: Some visual puzzle questions may rely on shapes, layout, or SVG graphics. We aim to keep these prompts understandable, but some may be less accessible to visitors using assistive technology. We will continue improving alternatives and wording.

sections.heading: Feedback
paragraphs: If you have difficulty using any page, quiz, or control, contact us with the page URL, your device/browser, and a description of the issue so we can investigate.

sections.heading: Overview
paragraphs: The Rainbow Hub provides quick quizzes, IQ-style challenges, and academic-style reasoning tests for entertainment and educational self-challenge. This Privacy Policy explains what information may be collected when you use the site, why it is used, and the choices you may have.
We do not ask you to create an account, and the quiz experience is designed to work without you directly submitting your name, email address, or other contact details.

sections.heading: Information We Collect
paragraphs: We may collect the following categories of information:
list: Device and browser information, such as browser type, operating system, screen size, language, and approximate location derived from IP address.
Usage information, such as pages viewed, quiz pages opened, buttons clicked, approximate session duration, and referring pages.
Advertising and measurement identifiers, such as cookie IDs, pixels, device identifiers, and similar technologies used by advertising and analytics partners.
Quiz interaction data, such as selected answers, completion events, score ranges, and whether a result or review screen was reached.
Contact information only if you voluntarily contact us, such as your email address and the contents of your message.

sections.heading: How We Use Information
list: To operate, maintain, secure, and improve the website.
To show quiz results, track progress during a quiz session, and improve quiz content.
To measure traffic, performance, errors, and user engagement.
To serve, limit, personalize, and measure advertising, including rewarded ads where available.
To understand whether campaigns and referrals are effective.
To respond to messages, legal requests, and abuse or security issues.

sections.heading: Advertising, Analytics, and Pixels
paragraphs: The site may use Google Publisher Tag, Google Ads/Google tag, Meta Pixel, and Assertive Yield. These services may set or read cookies and similar identifiers, collect device and usage information, and process data to deliver ads, measure ad performance, prevent fraud, and report analytics.

Meta Pixel is configured with pixel ID 843363384736830. Google tag is configured with advertising tag AW-18146485081. Google Ad Manager may be used for display or rewarded advertising. Assertive Yield may be used for advertising operations and yield measurement.

sections.heading: Cookies and Similar Technologies
paragraphs: Cookies, pixels, local storage, tags, and SDK-like scripts may be used to remember basic preferences, measure site performance, support advertising, and understand how visitors use the site. See our Cookie Policy for more detail.

sections.heading: Sharing Information
paragraphs: We may share information with:
list: Advertising and analytics providers that help operate, measure, or monetize the site.
Hosting, security, debugging, and infrastructure providers.
Professional advisers, regulators, law enforcement, or courts where required by law or necessary to protect rights and safety.
A successor organization if the site or its assets are sold, merged, or transferred.
after: We do not sell personal information that you directly submit through an account system, because the site does not currently provide accounts.

sections.heading: Your Choices
list: You can block or delete cookies in your browser settings.
You can use browser privacy controls, private browsing, or tracking-prevention settings.
You can adjust ad personalization settings through Google, Meta, and other advertising platforms where available.
You can contact us to request access, correction, deletion, or restriction where these rights apply under your local law.

sections.heading: Children
paragraphs: The site is intended for a general audience and is not directed to children under 13. If you believe a child has provided personal information, contact us so we can review and delete it where appropriate.

sections.heading: International Visitors
paragraphs: The site may be accessed from different countries. Data may be processed in countries where our service providers operate. Those countries may have privacy laws different from your own.

sections.heading: Retention
paragraphs: We retain information only as long as reasonably needed for the purposes described above, unless a longer period is required for legal, security, accounting, or operational reasons.

sections.heading: Contact
paragraphs: For privacy questions or requests, use the contact details on our Contact page.

sections.heading: What Cookies Are
paragraphs: Cookies are small files stored by your browser. Similar technologies include pixels, tags, local storage, scripts, and device identifiers. They help websites work, remember limited information, measure performance, and support advertising.

sections.heading: How We Use Them
list: Essential technologies: needed for basic site operation, security, routing, and quiz functionality.
Analytics technologies: used to understand visits, page views, clicks, and performance.
Advertising technologies: used to serve ads, measure impressions, limit fraud, and support rewarded ad flows.
Local storage: may be used to keep temporary quiz progress or client-side state during a session.

sections.heading: Third-Party Technologies
paragraphs: The site may load Google Publisher Tag, Google tag, Meta Pixel, and Assertive Yield. These providers may set or read cookies and similar identifiers according to their own policies.

sections.heading: Your Controls
paragraphs: You can block, delete, or restrict cookies in your browser settings. Some browsers also provide tracking prevention, private browsing, global privacy controls, or extension-based blocking. If you disable cookies or scripts, some ads, analytics, or quiz features may not work as intended.

sections.heading: Consent and Regional Requirements
paragraphs: Depending on where visitors are located, the site may use a cookie consent banner or consent management platform for non-essential analytics and advertising cookies. Consent tools should be configured for the regions and advertising partners that apply to the site.

sections.heading: Acceptance of These Terms
paragraphs: By using The Rainbow Hub, you agree to these Terms of Use. If you do not agree, do not use the site. These terms apply to all quiz pages, homepage content, legal pages, scripts, advertisements, and related features.

sections.heading: Entertainment and Educational Use Only
paragraphs: Quizzes, IQ-style scores, Oxford-style or Cambridge-style profiles, pass rates, rankings, and explanations are provided for entertainment, general education, and self-challenge only. They are not official assessments and must not be treated as medical, psychological, educational, employment, admissions, legal, or financial advice.

sections.heading: No Affiliation With Universities
paragraphs: References to Oxford, Cambridge, Harvard, or other academic-style names are descriptive of quiz themes only. The site is not affiliated with, endorsed by, sponsored by, or officially connected to any university, admissions office, testing body, or school unless explicitly stated in writing.

sections.heading: Permitted Use
list: Use the site for personal, lawful, non-commercial quiz taking.
Do not attempt to interfere with the site, ads, scripts, hosting, or security controls.
Do not scrape, copy, republish, sell, or create derivative quiz databases without permission.
Do not use the site to make decisions about another person’s education, employment, health, or eligibility.

sections.heading: Advertising and Third-Party Services
paragraphs: The site may display advertising, including rewarded ads. Third-party services may operate under their own terms and privacy policies. We are not responsible for third-party websites, ads, scripts, offers, or content that we do not control.

sections.heading: Intellectual Property
paragraphs: The site design, quiz text, explanations, code, graphics, and branding are owned by the site operator or licensed to the site. You may not reproduce or exploit them except as allowed by law or with written permission.

sections.heading: Disclaimers
paragraphs: The site is provided “as is” and “as available.” We do not guarantee uninterrupted access, error-free operation, exact score accuracy, ad availability, or that every quiz question will be suitable for every visitor.

sections.heading: Limitation of Liability
paragraphs: To the fullest extent allowed by law, we are not liable for indirect, incidental, special, consequential, exemplary, or punitive damages arising from your use of the site. Nothing in these terms limits liability where doing so would be unlawful.

sections.heading: Changes
paragraphs: We may update these terms from time to time. Continued use of the site after changes are posted means you accept the updated terms.

sections.heading: Informal Online Challenges
paragraphs: The Rainbow Hub publishes quick quizzes for entertainment, curiosity, and self-challenge. They are designed to be fun, accessible, and easy to play on a phone or desktop.
They are not official IQ tests, admissions tests, academic exams, psychological assessments, medical tools, or professional evaluations.

sections.heading: Scores Are Estimates
paragraphs: Scores, profiles, pass rates, result tiers, and percentile-style labels are based only on the questions inside each quiz. They are broad entertainment feedback, not precise measurements of intelligence, knowledge, health, education, or future performance.
A high or low score should not be treated as proof of ability. Different question sets, languages, devices, distractions, and reading speed can all affect results.

sections.heading: Academic Names and Themes
paragraphs: Quizzes that use names such as Oxford, Cambridge, Harvard, Stanford, Princeton, or similar academic themes are inspired by the idea of challenging reasoning and selective study environments.
Those names do not mean that the quiz is created, approved, endorsed, sponsored, or reviewed by any university, school, admissions office, testing provider, or public institution.

sections.heading: Pass Rates and Difficulty Claims
paragraphs: Claims such as “only 3% can pass” are entertainment-style challenge labels. They are intended to make the quiz feel competitive and engaging, not to report an independently verified statistic.
Actual results will vary depending on the audience, quiz version, language, and how people interact with the site.

sections.heading: No Decision-Making Use
paragraphs: Do not use quiz results to make important decisions about education, admissions, employment, health, diagnosis, eligibility, finances, or a person’s abilities.
If you need a formal assessment, use an official testing body, a qualified professional, a school, university, employer, doctor, or other appropriate authority.

sections.heading: Questions, Translations, and Accuracy
paragraphs: We aim to make questions clear, fair, and translation-friendly, but mistakes can happen. A question may contain an error, a translation may lose nuance, or an explanation may be incomplete.
We may update questions, answers, explanations, titles, difficulty labels, and result profiles over time.

sections.heading: Advertising and Rewarded Access
paragraphs: Some quiz steps may include adverts, sponsored placements, or rewarded-ad gates. Watching an advert may unlock the next stage or a result screen, but it does not change your score, answers, profile, or result tier.
Advertising availability may depend on location, device, network, consent choices, or third-party ad systems.

sections.heading: Progress and Local Data
paragraphs: The site may use browser storage to remember quiz progress, answers, or a recently viewed stage so a quiz can resume after a refresh. Clearing browser data or using another device may reset that progress.
For privacy details, cookies, analytics, adverts, and similar technologies, please read the Privacy Policy and Cookie Policy.
